tasks.title: Dark mode WCAG AA contrast fixes across remaining components
tasks.description:
Wave 21 fixed dark mode contrast in TaskDetailPanel, Button, GlobalSearchModal, and ProjectDetail but left other components untouched. Audit and fix `dark:text-slate-400` on dark backgrounds where it violates WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast (slate-400 on slate-900 = ~3.5:1).

Specific fixes needed — change `dark:text-slate-400` to `dark:text-slate-300` in these contexts:
- **BacklogView.tsx** lines 239, 305: button text ('Create Sprint', 'Load more tasks')
- **KanbanBoard.tsx** line 224: badge count text on `dark:bg-slate-700` background
- **CalendarView.tsx** lines 80, 86, 92: navigation button text (prev/next/today); line 103: day header labels
- **CSVImportModal.tsx** line 246: close button text
- **CloseSprintModal.tsx** lines 265, 295, 309: button text ('Cancel', 'Go to Backlog')
- **SprintTransitionModal.tsx** line 171: close button text
- **ProjectSettingsModal.tsx** lines 425, 431, 437, 443: inactive tab button text
- **ProjectDashboard.tsx** line 43: status label text

Do NOT change `dark:text-slate-400` where it's used for form labels, placeholder hints, or decorative/secondary text — only fix it for readable text on interactive elements and dark backgrounds. Keep changes minimal — only touch the specific lines identified.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: All button text, tab text, and badge text on dark backgrounds uses dark:text-slate-300 or lighter. pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint passes. Visual: no pale-on-dark unreadable text remains in the 8 files listed.

tasks.title: Fix all pre-existing React lint warnings (0 errors → 0 warnings)
tasks.description:
There are 9 lint warnings across 6 files, all in the web package. Fix every single one:

1. **BurndownChart.tsx:60** (`react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`) — `fetchData()` called directly in useEffect. Fix: inline the fetch logic into the effect body instead of calling the useCallback, or restructure so the setState calls happen inside `.then()` callbacks that the effect schedules (not synchronous setState in the effect body).

2. **GanttChart.tsx:148** (`react-hooks/exhaustive-deps`, 2 warnings) — `const allTasks = [...datedTasks, ...noDateTasks]` creates a new array every render, making useMemo deps at lines 156 and 215 change each render. Fix: wrap `allTasks` in its own `useMemo(() => [...datedTasks, ...noDateTasks], [datedTasks, noDateTasks])` before the dependent useMemos.

3. **ProjectSettingsModal.tsx:106** (`react-hooks/exhaustive-deps`) — `loadData` missing from useEffect deps. Fix: either wrap `loadData` in useCallback with `[projectId]` deps and add it to the effect deps, or inline the logic.

4. **DropdownMenu.tsx:39** (`react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`) — `setActiveIndex(0)` called synchronously in effect. Fix: move the index reset into the state toggle that sets `open` to true (i.e., when `setOpen(true)` is called, also `setActiveIndex(0)` at that point), then remove it from the effect.

5. **TaskPlanApprovalDialog.tsx:317** (`react/no-unescaped-entities`) — unescaped apostrophe in JSX text. Replace `You've` with `You've` or use a JS string expression `{"You've rejected..."}`.

6. **Home.tsx:38,41** (`react/no-unescaped-entities`, 2 warnings) — unescaped apostrophes. Line 38: `What's` → `What's`. Line 41: `we'll` → `we'll`.

7. **Projects.tsx:29** (`react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`) — `fetchProjects()` called directly in effect. Fix: same pattern as BurndownChart — inline the fetch or restructure so setState doesn't happen synchronously in the effect body.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: pnpm lint shows 0 errors AND 0 warnings across all packages. No behavioral changes — all existing functionality preserved. pnpm typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Migrate inline pill patterns to Badge component across 6 key files
tasks.description: The `Badge` component (`apps/web/src/components/shared/Badge.tsx`) was created in Wave 22-23 with variants: success (green), warning (amber), danger (red), info (blue), neutral (slate). Many files still use inline pill patterns. Migrate the highest-impact files. Import `Badge` from `../shared/Badge` (or appropriate relative path) and replace inline patterns: **SprintSection.tsx:** - Line 349: `Active` → `Active` - Lines 188-192: Priority badges using `priorityStyles` record. Map: critical → danger, high → warning, medium → info, low → neutral. Due date badges use `dueDateColor()` — map overdue → danger, soon → warning, normal → neutral. **TableView.tsx:** - Lines 135 and 199-206: Two identical priority badge ternary chains (virtualized and non-virtualized). Create a helper `function priorityVariant(p: string)` that maps priority → Badge variant, then replace both with `{task.priority}`. **TaskFieldsPanel.tsx:** - Lines 26-28: `priorityColors` record. Replace the inline `` at the usage sites with Badge. **Search.tsx:** - Lines 18-22 `statusColors` record + line 156 status badge. Map: done → success, in_progress → info, in_review → warning, todo → neutral. **Portfolio.tsx:** - Lines 25-30 `healthBadge` function. Replace returned className strings with Badge variant: Healthy → success, At Risk → warning, Critical → danger, N/A → neutral. Note: Badge uses `rounded-full` and specific padding. Some existing pills use `rounded` (not full). The Badge component matches the audit's desired style. Where existing pills have extra classes like `flex-shrink-0`, pass them via `className` prop.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: All 5 files import and use Badge component instead of inline pill class strings. Priority/status/health indicators render identically to before (green for done/healthy/active, red for critical/overdue, etc.). pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint passes. No visual regressions.

tasks.title: Card component adoption + ProjectToolbar overlay positioning fix
tasks.description:
Two improvements:

**1. Card component migration in ProfilePage.tsx:**
Line 93 has: `<div className="bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 rounded-lg border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700 p-6 space-y-6">`. Replace with `<Card className="space-y-6">` (default `padding='md'` = p-6). Import Card from `../components/shared/Card`. There may be other card-like wrappers in the same file — check and migrate any that match the Card pattern.

**2. Fix ProjectToolbar template/export overlay positioning:**
Lines 290 and 338 both use `className="absolute right-16 top-12 ..."` for the template menu and export menu overlays. These have two problems:
- Both share identical positioning, so if both were somehow open they'd stack
- The `right-16 top-12` is hardcoded relative to the toolbar `<>` fragment, not to a positioned parent

Fix: Wrap each overlay's trigger+content in a `relative` positioned container (like the existing template menu pattern with `ref={templateMenuRef}`). Change positioning from `absolute right-16 top-12` to `absolute right-0 top-full mt-1` so they anchor to their trigger button. For the export menu, add a `ref` and click-outside/Escape handling (the template menu already has this via the existing `useEffect` at lines 54-73). Make sure both menus close each other when opened (set the other's state to false).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: ProfilePage uses Card component instead of inline card div. Template and export menus position correctly relative to their trigger buttons (not hardcoded offsets). Opening one menu closes the other. Escape and click-outside close both menus. pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint passes.

tasks.title: Replace window.confirm in useProjectData popstate handler with ConfirmDialog
tasks.description:
The last remaining native `window.confirm()` call is in `useProjectData.ts` lines 277-279 inside a `popstate` event handler that blocks navigation during AI generation. Replace it with the `useConfirmDialog` hook.

**Challenge:** The `popstate` handler is a plain DOM event listener (not React), so you can't `await` inside it. The solution is to use a state-based approach:

1. In `useProjectData.ts`, add state: `const [pendingNavAway, setPendingNavAway] = useState(false);`
2. In the `popstate` handler, instead of calling `window.confirm()`, set `setPendingNavAway(true)` and push state to prevent navigation.
3. Export `pendingNavAway` and a `confirmNavAway(confirmed: boolean)` callback from the hook. When `confirmed` is true: abort AI, call `window.history.back()`. When false: push state again.
4. In `ProjectDetail.tsx`, use `useConfirmDialog` hook and render `<ConfirmDialogPortal />`. When `d.pendingNavAway` becomes true, call `confirm({ title: 'Leave page?', message: 'An AI generation is in progress...', confirmLabel: 'Leave', variant: 'warning' })` and pass the result to `d.confirmNavAway(result)`.

This preserves the same UX (user sees a dialog asking to confirm leaving) but uses the branded ConfirmDialog instead of the native browser dialog.

Alternative simpler approach: Since `useConfirmDialog().confirm()` returns a Promise, you can call it from the popstate handler and use `.then()` instead of `await`. Store the `confirm` function in a ref so it's accessible from the event handler closure. This avoids needing to change the ProjectDetail component at all — but you still need to render `<ConfirmDialogPortal />` somewhere. Either approach is acceptable — choose whichever is cleaner.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: No native window.confirm() calls remain anywhere in the codebase (verify with grep). The AI generation navigation blocker still works: trying to navigate back during generation shows a branded ConfirmDialog. Confirming aborts generation and navigates away. Cancelling keeps the user on the page. pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint passes.

tasks.title: Expand shared-types re-exports in resolvers + S3 edge-case tests
tasks.description:
Two backend improvements:

**1. Shared-types re-exports in remaining resolvers:**
Sprint, task, and project resolvers already re-export from `@tasktoad/shared-types` (pattern: `export type { X as SharedX } from '@tasktoad/shared-types'`). Add the same pattern to resolvers that deal with types available in shared-types. Check `packages/shared-types/src/index.ts` to see which types are exported, then add re-exports to:
- `comment.ts` — if Comment type is in shared-types
- `notification.ts` — if Notification type is in shared-types
- `report.ts` — if Report type is in shared-types
Only add re-exports for types that actually exist in shared-types. If a type isn't there, skip that resolver.

**2. S3 unit test edge cases:**
Current tests cover happy paths well (14 tests). Add edge-case coverage to `s3.unit.test.ts`:
- **buildS3Key sanitization:** Test with filenames containing spaces, special chars, unicode. Verify the key format is `attachments/{orgId}/{taskId}/{timestamp}-{filename}`.
- **Custom S3 endpoint:** Mock `S3_ENDPOINT` env var and verify the S3Client is created with `endpoint` config and `forcePathStyle: true`.
- **uploadToS3 error handling:** Mock PutObjectCommand to throw a network error. Verify the error propagates (is NOT silently swallowed).
- **getSignedDownloadUrl with ATTACHMENT_URL_EXPIRY_SECONDS:** Mock the env var to a custom value (e.g., 3600) and verify it's passed to getSignedUrl.
- **deleteFromS3 error handling:** Mock DeleteObjectCommand to throw. Verify error propagates.

Keep tests focused and fast — mock at the AWS SDK level (already set up in the existing test file).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Resolver re-exports added for all applicable types from shared-types. S3 tests expanded with 5+ new edge-case tests. pnpm test passes. pnpm typecheck passes.
